cards.prompt: A Volatile.Read loop observes a Volatile.Write of the stop flag and exits; after Join the worker is dead (stopped=True, flag=1)
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; int stop = 0; var t = new Thread(() => { while (Volatile.Read(ref stop) == 0) { } }); t.Start(); Thread.Sleep(50); Volatile.Write(ref stop, 1); t.Join(); Console.WriteLine($"stopped={t.IsAlive == false} sawStop={Volatile.Read(ref stop)}");

cards.prompt: Volatile changes barrier/memory semantics, not the value: writing 42 then reading round-trips to 42
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; int x = 0; Volatile.Write(ref x, 42); int r = Volatile.Read(ref x); Console.WriteLine($"r={r}");

cards.prompt: A volatile write of the ready flag after data=99 publishes the data; a reader that observes the flag then reads data sees 99 (write barrier + read barrier)
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; int data = 0; int ready = 0; int seen = -1; var writer = new Thread(() => { data = 99; Volatile.Write(ref ready, 1); }); var reader = new Thread(() => { while (Volatile.Read(ref ready) == 0) {} seen = data; }); reader.Start(); writer.Start(); writer.Join(); reader.Join(); Console.WriteLine($"seen={seen}");
